questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Paragraph: EDTA is used as a complexing agent in chemical analysis. Solution of EDTA usually containing the disodium salt $\mathrm{Na}_2 \mathrm{H}_2$ EDTA are also used to treat heavy metal poisoning. EDTA is a hexadentate ligand having the following structure.
Question: A solution having equal moles of both reactant show the following equilibrium $\left(\mathrm{Keq}=10^{-3}\right)$ $\mathrm{Pb}^{2+}(\mathrm{aq})+\mathrm{H}_2 \operatorname{EDTA}^{2-}(\mathrm{aq}) \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{PbEDTA}^{2-}(\mathrm{aq})+2 \mathrm{H}^{+}(\mathrm{aq})$ If concentration of complex at equilibrium is $10^{-3} \mathrm{M}$ find the concentration of $\mathrm{Pb}^{2+}$ at equilibrium maintained at $\mathrm{pH}=3$
questions.solution:
Let conc. of both reactants at equilibrium is $\times \mathrm{M}$
$\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{Keq}=\frac{\left[\mathrm{PbEDTA}^{2-}\right]\left[\mathrm{H}^{+}\right]^2}{\mathrm{x}^2} \\ & \mathrm{x}=10^{-3}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

options: Because it is very weak field ligand
